properties.page_content:
WEB REFERENCE Reservations
*General Information
Policy and procedures for traveling with carry-on/cabin pets on domestic or international flights operated by
American Airlines/American Eagle.
 
 
Related Information
Related Topics Information and Links
Checked Pets (Checked Pets as Baggage) CHECKED PET
Customer Check-in Customer Check-in
Celebrity Animals CELEBRITY PET
Military/Police, Search/Rescue and Drug/Explosive
Detection Animals SPECIALTY ANIMALS
Service Animals and Puppies in Training for Service
Animals SPCL ASSIST ANIMALS
 
Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance Critieria:
American Airlines ONLY accepts cats and dogs as cabin pets
No other animal species is accepted as a cabin pet
Cabin pets must be well-mannered and free of odor, parasites and illnesses
Animals displaying any type of disruptive behavior will be refused transportation as a cabin pet.
Disruptive behavior includes, but is not limited to:
Barking/meowing constantly and/or loudly
Biting or attempting to bite
Growling/hissing
Scratching at or jumping on customers or employees
CABIN PET
10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 1/13

properties.summary: American Airlines accepts only cats and dogs as cabin pets, requiring them to be well-mannered, odor-free, and healthy. Disruptive behaviors like barking, biting, growling, or scratching result in refusal for transportation. Other animal species are not permitted as cabin pets.

properties.page_content:
Relieving themselves outside of designated animal relief area
Customers, both revenue and non-revenue, traveling with cabin pets are required
To check-in with an agent at the ticket counter
Complete the animal inspection process
Properly documented for travel to its final destination
Cabin pet MUST remain inside the closed/zipped kennel at all times, including:
Gate lounge area
During boarding and deplaning of the aircraft
All Admirals Clubs and Flagship Lounges 
Onboard the aircraft
There is no age requirement for passengers traveling with cabin pets
Travelers of any age, as long as they are in a seat (no infant in arms or lap child), can travel with a
cabin pet
Note:  Pet strollers are NOT PERMITTED for cabin pets. The pet stroller is consider checked baggage and all
applicable checked baggage fees apply
CDC High Risk Countries
U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) strives to protect families and pets by preventing the
reintroduction of dog rabies into the United States. 
American Airlines is not accepting Cabin Pet/Carry-on dogs from high-risk rabies countries, or visited high-
risk rabies countries in the last 6 months.  If the customer is traveling from any of the countries listed below
with a dog, advise unable to accept cabin pet. Exceptions are for service animals and for U.S. Military or State
Department Foreign Service Personnel on official orders traveling with cabin pet. Must comply with CDC
requirements. If you have a customer who says traveling with a cabin pet and is on U.S. Military or State
Department Foreign Service Personnel on official orders, see Reservation Action for handling. 
 The CDC rabies restricted countries for AA destinations.
Belize Cuba Dominican Republic Ecuador
El Salvador Guatemala Guyana Haiti
Honduras Peru Suriname  
Note: Customers traveling with dog(s) from a high-risk rabies country or visited a high-risk rabies country in
the last 6 months not listed in the AA destinations will not be accepted to enter the U.S. 
Note: Cats are accepted as Cabin Pet as they are not part of the high-risk for rabies. Reservation Action is in
place for those traveling with cats.  AACoRN cannot distinguish if it's a cat or dog when booking a cabin pet,
the booking for a cat as a cabin pet must be done by SAC. 
Reservation Action
Advise the customer the Cabin Pet for cats will be booked by a separate desk
Ask the customer if the Cabin Pet is a cat, if so proceed with the next step
Blind transfer to SAC during SAC hours
Except for MCLA to SAC Spanish and EUPA SAC - Warm Transfer 
SAC after hours
Document PNR historical remarks CABIN PET EXCEPTION
10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 2/13

document_metadata.creator: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/129.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
document_metadata.title: WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations

properties.summary: Passengers traveling with cabin pets must check in at the ticket counter, complete an animal inspection, and ensure pets remain in closed kennels at all times. Pet strollers are not allowed and are considered checked baggage with applicable fees. Dogs from CDC high-risk rabies countries or those visited in the last six months are not accepted, except for service animals or U.S. Military/State Department personnel on official orders. Cats are accepted as cabin pets since they are not high-risk for rabies, but bookings for cats must be handled by a separate desk. Customers traveling with cabin pets must comply with all CDC and airline requirements.
themes: Age requirements for passengers with pets
entities: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

properties.page_content:
Verify the phone contact for the customer
Queue place the PNR to DFW105/11
Advise the customer an AA Representative will be contacting them to assist with the arrangements
If the customer has a dog, advise dogs are not accepted until further notice
 
Note:  Always check the Destination Restrictions section to verify those destinations where AA policy does not
provide a PET IN CABIN option. 
 
 
CDC Screwworm
The U.S. Department of Agriculture Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service Veterinary Services (USDA
APHIS VS) regulates dogs from regions affected with screwworm. As per USDA APHIS VS, dogs traveling
from countries or regions where screwworm is known to exist may enter the United States if they are
accompanied by a certificate signed by a full-time salaried veterinary official of the region of origin stating:
If the dog is traveling from a High-Risk Rabies country see list below, the dog cannot travel
The dog has been inspected for screwworm within five (5) days prior to shipment to the United States
The dog is either free from screwworm 
OR
The dog was infested with screwworm, held in quarantine, and treated until free from screwworm prior to
leaving the region
 
Reservation Action
Advise the customer the Cabin Pet will have to be booked by a separate desk
Blind transfer to SAC during SAC hours
Except for MCLA to SAC Spanish - Warm Transfer 
SAC after hours
Document PNR historical remarks CABIN PET EXCEPTION
Verify the phone contact for the customer
Queue place the PNR to DFW105/11
Advise the customer an AA Representative will be contacting them to assist with the arrangements
 
Screwworm restricted countries for AA destinations; allows Cabin Pet travel.
Aruba Costa Rica Cuba Dominican
Republic Ecuador Guyana
Haiti Nicaragua Panama Peru Trinidad and
Tobago  
Age Restrictions
Pets are required to be a minimum of eight (8) weeks old for American Airlines:
Domestic travel eight (8) weeks old
International travel minimum age maybe higher, check Web Timatic for minimum age

properties.summary: Verify customer contact details and inform them an AA Representative will assist with arrangements. Dogs from screwworm-affected regions require a veterinary certificate for U.S. entry, confirming inspection and treatment if necessary. High-risk rabies countries prohibit dog travel. Cabin pets must be booked separately, with specific transfer protocols and historical remarks documented. Screwworm-restricted countries allow cabin pet travel, and pets must meet age requirements: eight weeks for domestic travel and higher for international travel.
entities: U.S. Department of Agriculture Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service Veterinary Services

properties.page_content:
Additional requirements may apply based on the customer's itinerary
IATA Live Animal Regulations prohibit cats and dogs younger than eight (8) weeks old being accepted for
transportation
No maximum age limit for travel
 
Additional Minimum Age Requirements
International itineraries
Representatives are required to verify pet
travel requirements in Web Timatic for each
country (including entry into the United States)
Entry to the United States
Entry requirements for dogs entry the U.S.
depends on the origin country's rabies
Verify and advise the customer of the Rabies
Risk Level assigned by the CDC 
Periodically the CDC updates a country's
assigned risk level, advise customer to
continue checking the website prior to their
departure for possible changes
Entry into Hawaii
Per AA Policy cabin pets are NEVER
PERMITTED on flights to/from any Hawaiian
destination
See Destination Restrictions for full details
 
Health Requirements
Health requirements for cabin pets:
Animals are visually examined at the airport during the check-in process
Animals must be free of odor, parasites and illness
Sedation - as recommended by the American Veterinary Medical Association (AVMA), AA does not accept
pets that have been sedated or tranquilized. Pets that have been given sedatives or tranquilizers are at a
higher risk of respiratory and cardiovascular problems at high altitudes
Domestic travel - A health certificate is not required for cabin pets however;
Pets must, have the proper health documentation for final destination if required
Customers should contact their veterinarian, state or country animal health commissions for
destination requirements
International travel - See Web Timatic for health certificate requirements and additional information
 
Note: A Pet Passport is not accepted in lieu of American's required documents. 
Destination Restrictions
Provided all requirements are met, AA only accepts cabin pets for travel to/from the following:
48 contiguous United States
10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 4/13

properties.summary: American Airlines has specific requirements for pet travel, including minimum age limits, health checks, and destination restrictions. Cats and dogs must be at least eight weeks old, and pets are visually examined at check-in to ensure they are free of odor, parasites, and illness. Sedated pets are not accepted due to health risks at high altitudes. Domestic travel does not require a health certificate, but international travel may require additional documentation verified through Web Timatic. Pets are not allowed on flights to or from Hawaii, and a Pet Passport is not accepted in place of required documents. Customers should check the CDC's rabies risk level for their destination and verify requirements periodically. Cabin pets are only accepted for travel to/from the 48 contiguous United States and other specified destinations.
themes: CDC updates on rabies risk levels
Sedation risks for pets during travel

Seating Restrictions
Cabin pets are restricted from certain locations and cabins depending on the aircraft. For example, lie-flat
seating in premium cabins does not have under seat storage capacity to allow space for pet kennel storage.
The following chart provides the cabin pet seating restrictions based on location or aircraft type:  
Aircraft Cabins / Rows Notes
All Aircraft Bulkhead Rows Cabin pets NOT PERMITTED - no
under seat storage space
All Aircraft Exit Rows Cabin pets NOT PERMITTED - FAA
safety requirement
A321T
(Transcon 3-cabin)
First & Business Aircraft also decoded as "32B" or
"A321 Sharklets"

properties.summary: American Airlines restricts cabin pet travel to certain regions, including Transatlantic, Transpacific, Hawaii, Jamaica, and South America, with specific limitations based on country and flight direction. Cabin pets are not permitted in bulkhead or exit rows due to storage and FAA safety requirements. Premium cabins with lie-flat seating lack under-seat storage for pet kennels. Exceptions apply for flights from Jamaica, where cabin pets are allowed. Restrictions vary by aircraft type and seating configuration.

NOTE: Service Animals (SVAN) are permitted in all cabins on all aircraft. They are permitted in bulkhead rows
but NEVER PERMITTED in Exit Rows.  On American Airlines two-aisle aircraft, seats in the center section
between the two aisles are not included as designated exit row seats. 
For complete details, see:

Kennel Requirements
Customers are responsible for providing an appropriate sized kennel/carrier for their cabin pet.  To be accepted
at the airport, the pet kennel must meet all of the following requirements:

properties.summary: Cabin pet kennels are available in First and Business Class with restrictions on size, weight, and placement during flight phases. Pets are not permitted in First or Business Class on certain aircraft due to lack of under-seat storage. Service animals are allowed in all cabins but not in exit rows. Customers must provide kennels meeting airline requirements for pet travel.
themes: Weight limit for pet and kennel
Service animals permitted in all cabins
Exit row restrictions for service animals

properties.page_content:
Kennel Area Requirements
 General
Be secure and fit under the seat on each aircraft type in the
customer's itinerary
Remain closed/zipped, with the animal inside the kennel,
and under the seat in front at all times during flight and
when requested by flight crew
Customer's with pet kennels are NEVER PERMITTED to sit in
bulkhead or exit row seating
 Space
REGULATORY: USDA Live Animal Regulations require the
pet must fit comfortably inside their kennels
Airport agents will verify space requirements
If the pet and/or kennel do not meet these requirements,
the pet will not be accepted for travel as a cabin pet
 Carry-on
Cabin pet kennel can carry-on a personal item or a carry-on
bag
Example: Cabin pet kennel + personal item or Cabin
pet kennel + carry-on bag 
 
Ventilation Kennel must have ventilation openings on three (3) or more
sides
 Material
 Hard-sided Kennels:
Must be constructed of wood, metal, rigid plastic or
composite material of similar strength
Door/opening must fasten/close securely and have easily
accessible openings for emergency removal, if necessary
Must be leak-proof and escape-proof 
 Soft-Sided Kennels:
Maximum: 18in length x 11in width x 11in height
Must be constructed of water-repellent, padded nylon with
mesh ventilation
Must contain absorbent material, such as a blanket or
towel, to prevent leakage
Weight Only restriction is on A321T in First and Business combine
weight of pet and kennel must be 20 lbs or less
Size See Kennel Size
 
Kennel Size
The maximum size of cabin pet kennel/carrier allowed depends on the operating aircraft in the customer's
itinerary. The chart below provides the maximum kennel size based on aircraft fleet:
Maximum Allowed Pet Kennel/Carrier Size      
 Aircraft Length Width Height

properties.summary: Kennel requirements for cabin pets include secure, ventilated kennels that fit under the seat and comply with USDA regulations. Hard-sided kennels must be durable and escape-proof, while soft-sided kennels should be water-repellent and padded. Pets and kennels must meet size and weight restrictions, with specific dimensions varying by aircraft type. Customers with kennels cannot sit in bulkhead or exit rows, and the kennel must remain closed during the flight. Carry-on rules allow a kennel plus a personal item or carry-on bag. Airport agents verify compliance, and non-compliant pets will not be accepted for travel.

Note: When quoting kennel size to the customer, always remember to discuss the kennel space requirements.
Regulations require to advise cabin pets must fit comfortably inside their kennels.
Landline Bus
Pet acceptance on Landline Bus:
 
Cargo Pets (Checked Pets) are not accepted on Landline, due to limitations
Cabin Pets (Carryon Pets) are accepted on Landline with a maximum of 4 cabin pets per vehicle
SSR PETC
Maximum Number Accepted
Below is the number of cabin pets and kennels permitted:
Maximum of one (1) carry-on kennel per customer
Exception made if customer purchases an extra seat
Purchase of extra seat will allow a maximum of two (2) carry-on kennels 
Two (2) pets may travel in the same kennel provided both pets meet ALL of the following requirements: 
Are similar size/weight
Are the same species (e.g., 2 cats or 2 dogs)
Both pets can turn about normally while standing, stand and sit erect, and can lie in a natural position
while inside the closed/zipped kennel without touching or protruding through the sides or top of the
kennel

properties.summary: Pet travel regulations specify kennel size requirements and comfort for cabin pets. Landline buses accept up to 4 cabin pets per vehicle but do not allow cargo pets. Customers can bring one carry-on kennel, with exceptions for an extra seat purchase allowing two kennels. Two pets of the same species and similar size can share a kennel if they can move comfortably inside. Aircraft restrictions vary by type and cabin class, with limits on the number of kennels permitted. Premium cabins on certain aircraft do not allow cabin pets, while main cabins have specific limits ranging from 2 to 5 kennels.
themes: Extra seat purchase for additional kennels

properties.summary: Regional aircraft include CRJ-700, CRJ-900, ERJ-170, ERJ-175, and ERJ-145, with varying limits on pet kennels per flight. Cabin pet fees are $150 USD for tickets issued on or after 20FEB24, and $125 USD for tickets issued before this date. Fees are exempt for travel from Cuba due to payment restrictions, requiring a waiver from RSD. Cabin pets are not interlined between American Airlines and other airlines, and fees must be paid separately for each airline. The pet kennel counts as a carry-on bag, and customers are allowed one personal item alongside the kennel. Additional carry-ons must be checked, with applicable baggage fees. Payment for pet fees can be made at the Ticket Counter or Ticket Sales Center using cash (where accepted), credit card, or MCO.

properties.page_content:
Fee applies from origin to destination unless a
connection is over four hours for domestic travel or
over 24 hours for international travel, then the
charge is per segment.
For tickets issued on/after 20FEB24 150 USD
applies:
One way
Per kennel
Note: 125 USD for tickets issued before 20FEB24.
Extra Seat
Customers may purchase an extra seat for a cabin pet:
Customers are permitted to purchase an extra seat to stow a cabin pet kennel/carrier provided the kennel
is stored and remains under the seat in front of the extra seat purchased. The kennel and/or pet should
never be placed or remain on the seat cushion of the extra seat at anytime
Applicable cabin pet fees apply in addition to the extra seat charge
Purchasing an extra seat does not provide additional carry-on or checked baggage allowance
Refer to AACORN NAV S for extra seat instructions; then
Refer to AACORN NAV P for steps to book the cabin pet
 
Note: Extra Seat policies apply. For example, Basic Economy is not qualify for Extra Seat.  For more details see
Web Reference file, Extra Seat. 
Employee Travel
See Employee Travel for policy and procedures for Cabin Pet. 
PNR Handling & Navigation
The following provides handling of pet PNR and important items:
 Handling of Pet PNR and Important Items
A pet PNR must be created for a cabin pet:
 
See AACORN NAV P for pet bookings steps
For travel within 48 U.S., Alaska, USVI and
Puerto Rico (PR) will go through the digital
path from AACoRN
When complete, the pet PNR will contain:
Last Name: PET
First Name: CBBG PASSENGERS LAST NAME
Example: PET/CBBG JONES
Remarks to cross-reference with the original
passenger PNR 
The system will also: Add an OSI to the original passenger PNR to
cross-reference with the pet PNR
10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 10/13

document_metadata.title: WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations

properties.summary: Pet travel policies include fees per segment unless connections exceed four hours domestically or 24 hours internationally. Cabin pet fees are $150 for tickets issued on/after 20FEB24 and $125 for tickets issued before. Customers can purchase an extra seat for cabin pets, but kennels must remain under the seat and not on the seat cushion. Extra seat charges do not increase baggage allowances, and Basic Economy is excluded. Pet PNRs must be created for cabin pets, with specific naming conventions and cross-referencing with passenger PNRs. Refer to AACORN NAV for booking and handling instructions.
themes: Employee travel policies for cabin pets

Web reference for extra seat policies

Curbside check-in is not allowed
Minimum check-in time applies
Customers should arrive to the airport at least two (2) hours prior to scheduled departure to allow
appropriate time to complete check-in for their pet
Maximum check-in time is 4 hours before scheduled departure

properties.summary: To manage pets in cabin (PETC), cancel the Pet PNR if the customer is no longer traveling or changing flights. Retrieve the pet PNR via the OSI field or by searching with the passenger's name. Create a new Pet PNR for upgraded cabins, ensuring seat restrictions are checked. If upgraded at the airport, agents handle the process, and pets may need to travel in the main cabin if space is unavailable. Pets must be checked in at the ticket counter, not curbside, with minimum and maximum check-in times enforced. Customers should arrive at least two hours before departure for pet check-in.

properties.page_content:
aa.com
 Cabin Pet can be added on aa.com up to 3 hours prior to departure.
Only 1 passenger at a time can add a Cabin Pet to the reservation. If additional passengers want to
request a Cabin pet, the process flow can be repeated for each passenger.
PNR cannot be changed online and passenger cannot Check in online when reservation contains a PETC
SSR.
 
When viewing an eligible booking online, customers will see the "Pet in cabin" option listed under Additional
Services.
Once the pet is added, a message will display and advise the customer of the kennel size requirements and
service fee which must be collected at the airport.
 
Keywords

petc, Hawaii, cdc, embargo, screwworm, pet, passport

properties.summary: Passengers can add a Cabin Pet to their reservation on aa.com up to 3 hours before departure, with one pet per passenger allowed. Online PNR changes and check-in are restricted for reservations containing a PETC SSR. Eligible bookings display the "Pet in cabin" option under Additional Services, and customers are informed of kennel size requirements and service fees at the airport. Updates include changes to pet fees, embargoes for high-risk countries, and cosmetic adjustments.
themes: Embargo on pets from high-risk countries
